title: Salesforce Flow: Stamp Lead on Account During Conversion
description:
## Context

Salesforce natively stamps the Lead with the Account on conversion (via ConvertedAccountId), but does not create the reverse relationship. Having the Account stamped with the originating Lead enables powerful reporting: slicing and dicing opportunities based on when and where the lead originated.

## Behavior

Create a Salesforce flow that, on lead conversion, populates a custom field on the Account object (`Converted_From_Lead__c`) with the lead ID. This creates a bidirectional foreign relationship between Lead and Account.

## Outcome

Upon investigation, the team discovered this functionality was already implemented by an existing flow using the `Converted_From_Lead__c` custom field on the Account object. The ticket was completed as the requirement was already met.
acceptance_criteria: Account object has a Converted_From_Lead__c field that references the originating lead
On lead conversion, the Account's Converted_From_Lead__c is populated with the lead ID
Bidirectional relationship exists: Lead -> Account (native) and Account -> Lead (custom)
middleware_changes: Salesforce flow on lead conversion that stamps the Account with the originating Lead via the Converted_From_Lead__c custom field. Investigation revealed this was already implemented by a prior flow.
data_model_changes: Custom field Converted_From_Lead__c on the Account object (already existed from prior implementation).
implementation_notes: Developer investigated and found the functionality was already covered by an existing flow. Salesforce natively provides ConvertedAccountId, ConvertedContactId, and ConvertedOpportunityId on the Lead object after conversion. The custom Converted_From_Lead__c field on Account completes the bidirectional relationship.
comment_summary: Developer explained that Salesforce already stamps Lead with Account (ConvertedAccountId) natively, and the custom Converted_From_Lead__c field on Account was already being populated by an existing flow. The requirement was already met.
